name: AI Customizer Manager
description: Creates, improves, versions, and classifies customs in the AI Customizer catalog.

versions.changelog: Initial release — catalog-aware manager for skills, agents, and patches.

Project inference from cwd (suggestion mode); patch auto-detection with .original baseline + blocking validations; gentle-ai detection via <!-- gentle-ai:* --> tag scan; guided exploration protocol (no subagent delegation, read-only outside write scope); agent creation collaborative checklist (Paso 2.10).

Skill creation now produces proper YAML frontmatter per tool: Claude gets full schema (name, description, optional when_to_use/paths/allowed-tools/etc.) for semantic auto-activation and paths-based triggers; Opencode gets reduced schema (name, description, optional license/compatibility/metadata) — Claude-only fields stripped from the mirror to avoid misleading readers. New Paso 2.11 collaborative questions for skill frontmatter (description + optional paths). Paso 4.3 rewritten with the mandatory frontmatter block and writing guidance (front-load use case, 1536-char budget, paths is Claude-only).

Paso 2.11 rewritten: manager no longer asks the user for the skill's description or paths verbatim. Instead it gathers requirements conversationally (what the skill does, when it fires, file-match vs semantic scope), DRAFTS the frontmatter itself, and PROPOSES it for field-level confirmation/correction before Show-before-write. Same propose-don't-decide pattern as patch auto-detection and project inference. Draft rules added: description front-loads WHEN + WHAT under 180 chars target, paths inferred from stated trigger only (narrow first), Claude and Opencode descriptions stay identical.

Three simultaneous changes to make the manager unambiguously English and collaboratively universal. (1) Language fix: all 'Paso'/'pasos' (Spanish) renamed to 'Step'/'steps' throughout both tool bodies and the intro index. No other Spanish leaks remained after audit. (2) New Step 0.7 Language policy: artifacts you write to disk are ALWAYS simple English (non-negotiable); communication WITH the user uses the user's language (English artifact fragments stay verbatim inside a non-English explanation). (3) New Step 1.7 Universal propose-don't-decide pattern: codifies the five-beat arc (free expression → incremental questions with free-expression fallbacks → draft + propose → confirm/correct iterate → persist) that ALL Step 2 operations now follow uniformly. Step 2.8 Analyst mode rewritten to invoke the universal arc explicitly for improve/classify/adapt-to-tool/delete, each with its own concrete proposal shape (diff-style summary per op).

Agent invocation symmetry across tools. Step 2.10 checklist grows from 9 to 11 dimensions: (10) Opencode mode primary vs subagent — asked explicitly instead of always defaulting to primary; (11) Claude slash-command companion — opt-in second asset `v<ver>/claude/slash-command.md` that the installer copies to `<claude>/commands/<id>.md`. Step 4.4 agent template rewritten with the new optional companion file, its minimal body shape (delegates to the subagent), and a cross-tool invocation symmetry table pairing slash-command with mode: primary and bare subagent body with mode: subagent. No manifest schema change — installer detects the companion by file presence. The general installer now handles multi-asset agents, retiring the previous 'slash commands are a manager-only privilege' gap.
